task:
Fix ticket 0076: the runner's idle continuation nudge can fire (pressuring Oscar to write the next directive) while the run is parked on an unanswered founder decision. Suppress that nudge while awaiting a founder decision, reusing the SAME awaiting-founder state the run already tracks — do NOT add a parallel predicate.

ROOT CAUSE (verified by Oscar, do not re-litigate):
- File `packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts`. The directive loop, when a prior `ask-founder-continue` directive set `pendingFounderContinue` (line ~1056/1307), loops back to the top, sets status to 'awaiting-founder' (line ~1308-1313), and then still calls `awaitOscarWithNudgeWatchdog('directive', ...)` (line ~1332) to wait for the next directive.
- Inside `awaitOscarWithNudgeWatchdog` (defined ~line 911), the monitor's `judge` (line ~940-956) returns `{ state: 'stuck', nudge: OSCAR_IDLE_NUDGE }` whenever `hasDebWatcher && sample.idleStreak > 0` (line ~953-954). It has NO awareness that the loop is parked on a founder decision, so the "You've gone quiet — write the next directive ... or wrap up" nudge (OSCAR_IDLE_NUDGE, line ~273) fires and pressures forward motion past the unanswered founder decision. That is exactly what happened in run_261.

REQUIRED CHANGE:
1. Thread an `awaitingFounder: boolean` into `awaitOscarWithNudgeWatchdog` (add it as a parameter). At the directive call site (~line 1332) pass `founderContinueWait !== null`. At the verify call site (the `awaitOscarWithNudgeWatchdog` reference passed into agent-step, ~line 1511) pass `false` (verify is never in a founder-wait state) — wire it through agent-step.ts's call signature so verify keeps today's behavior unchanged.
   - `founderContinueWait !== null` IS the in-process driver of the 'awaiting-founder' run status (it is what selects 'awaiting-founder' vs 'awaiting-directive' at line ~1308-1309), i.e. the SAME awaiting-founder state class the daemon `ticketCloseGate` keys off. Reuse it; do NOT invent a new predicate or read a second source of truth.
2. In the judge inside `awaitOscarWithNudgeWatchdog`: when `awaitingFounder` is true, the generic idle path must NOT return the OSCAR_IDLE_NUDGE stuck state — return `{ state: 'progressing' }` instead so no forward-pressure nudge is sent. The Oz-authored-nudge and founder-stop branches above it must keep working unchanged (founder-stop still settles; Oz nudges are a separate ADR-0016/0017 channel and are out of scope for this fix — leave them as-is).
3. Do NOT change verify-stage behavior, the Bob build-watch monitor, the founder-stop path, or the directive-timeout fault wording. Keep the diff minimal and on the idle-nudge gating only.

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK:
- The existing Oscar idle-stall detection for a genuinely idle run that is NOT awaiting a founder decision (the `gatedStallHarness` family in `packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts`) must still fire exactly one nudge. Run that suite and confirm it stays green.
- The existing `ask-founder-continue` happy-path test (~line 855-895 in runner.test.ts, asserting founder-decision-requested → second delegation → wrapup ordering and `status: 'completed'`) must stay green.

REGRESSION TEST (required, add to `packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts`):
- Add a test proving that while the run is parked on a pending `ask-founder-continue` directive, NO idle continuation nudge is delivered to Oscar — i.e. no `oscar-nudge` event carrying the OSCAR_IDLE_NUDGE text ("You've gone quiet") is recorded for the awaiting-founder window, and Oscar's pane receives no "write the next directive ... or wrap up" idle prompt while parked. Model the harness on the existing `gatedStallHarness` (holds the Oscar screen CONSTANT while a directive is parked so idleStreak climbs) combined with the `askFounderContinue(...)` directive helper and a configured Deb watcher (`deb`) so `hasDebWatcher` is true — the same conditions under which the bug fires. The key assertion: under those exact idle conditions, because the parked directive is the one following an `ask-founder-continue`, the idle nudge is suppressed. Contrast it against / keep intact a sibling case where an ordinary (non-founder) parked directive still nudges, so the test proves the gate is specific to the awaiting-founder state and did not just disable idle detection globally.

EVIDENCE TO REPORT BACK:
- The exact files changed and a one-line description per change.
- The command(s) you ran to prove it: `pnpm --dir 'packages/core' test` (or the repo's test command for the core package / runner.test.ts) with the new test PASSING and the existing nudge + ask-founder-continue tests still green, plus `pnpm -w typecheck` (or the repo typecheck) clean. Paste the real pass/fail summary lines, not a claim.

WRITE SCOPE: `packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts`, `packages/core/src/runner/agent-step.ts` (only to thread the new param through the verify call signature), and `packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts`. Do not touch the daemon ticket-close-gate, prompts.ts wording, or any other file unless threading the parameter strictly requires it — if it does, name the file and why in your report.
